prerequisites: Platform must be Cisco 8000 (only platform supporting express reboot)
All critical containers running: database, swss, syncd, bgp
Sufficient disk space on /host (>1GB free for dump.rdb + sai-warmboot.bin)
No stale warmboot files from a previous failed reboot
Next boot image verified: sonic-installer list
ASIC config compatible between current and next image (MD5 match)
purpose: Perform a sub-second data plane downtime reboot on Cisco 8000 platforms by skipping PHY init and deferring NPU programming until config is restored

rollback.action: If express reboot aborted during pre-shutdown, the switch should still be running normally. Verify:
rollback.notes: Express reboot abort unfreezes orchagent and resumes normal operation. If containers are unhealthy, restart them: sudo systemctl restart swss syncd

rollback.action: If switch booted but reconciliation failed, restore config from backup

commands.command: sudo config load /tmp/config_backup_<timestamp>.json -y && sudo config reload -y

rollback.notes: Replace <timestamp> with the backup file from step 6. This restores CONFIG_DB and triggers a full config reload.

steps.notes: Express reboot requires hardware/SDK support only available on Cisco 8000.
If any critical container is down, fix that first before attempting express reboot.

verification.confirms_if: All four containers are in running state
verification.look_for: Count should be 4 (all critical containers running)

steps.action: Check for stale warmboot state from previous failed reboot
steps.notes: Stale warmboot files from a failed reboot can cause boot-up to use outdated state. Remove them: sudo rm -f /host/warmboot/dump.rdb /host/warmboot/sai-warmboot.bin

verification.confirms_if: No stale warmboot files and state is clean
verification.look_for: No stale files or state entries. If WARM_RESTART_TABLE shows a non-DISABLED state, previous reboot did not finalize.

steps.action: Verify disk space for warmboot files
steps.notes: dump.rdb size depends on route/FDB scale. Large deployments (100k+ routes) need more space.

verification.confirms_if: Available space is sufficient for dump.rdb (~50-500MB) and sai-warmboot.bin (~10-100MB)

steps.notes: If upgrading, ensure the next image was installed and set as default.

verification.confirms_if: A valid SONiC image is configured as next boot
verification.look_for: Next boot image is set and valid

steps.notes: Always backup before reboot. This captures running CONFIG_DB state.

daemon_impact.impact: Frozen during checkpoint, reconciles on boot-up
Pre-shutdown with express flag (-pxe), warm init on boot-up
Graceful restart triggered, sessions re-establish post-reboot
LAG keepalive packets sent during transition

steps.notes: Use -v for verbose output. The switch will kexec and come back up. Flags: -f (force), -d (skip DB check), -i (skip ASIC MD5 check). Verify: Script proceeds through pre-checks, freeze, checkpoint, and kexec without errors (confirms if No ERROR lines in verbose output; switch reboots)
Run this after the switch comes back up.

verification.confirms_if: Express reboot was the recorded cause

steps.notes: If state is not 'reconciled' after 2-3 minutes, check orchagent and syncd logs for stalls.

verification.look_for: Both orchagent and syncd state should be 'reconciled'

commands.command: show bgp summary; show interfaces status; show ip route summary

steps.notes: Compare BGP peer count, interface states, and route count against pre-reboot baseline.

verification.confirms_if: All expected BGP sessions are re-established
verification.look_for: BGP session count matches pre-reboot state

warnings: Express reboot is Cisco 8000 ONLY. Running on other platforms will be rejected.
Cross-version SDK upgrades may cause punt/inject header mismatches during the 1-2s transition window.
If the pre-shutdown checkpoint fails, the reboot aborts and the switch resumes normal operation.
Do NOT modify CONFIG_DB while a reboot is in progress.
Skipping the ASIC MD5 check (-i flag) risks incompatible ASIC state on boot-up.
